terms.definition: A share of ownership in a company. When you own a stock, you own a small piece of that business and may benefit if the company grows.
A short symbol (like AAPL or NVDA) used to identify a stock on exchanges. Tickers make it easy to look up prices and news.
Market capitalization = share price × total shares. It measures how big a company is in dollar terms.
How many shares traded in a period. High volume means many buyers and sellers were active.
Today's volume compared to recent average. Unusual activity (RVOL > 1) can signal attention or news.
A group of companies in the same industry (e.g. Technology, Energy, Financials). Events often affect whole sectors.
An event that could move a stock — earnings, FDA approval, merger, rate decision, or major news.
Official documents companies submit to the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission. Public and free via EDGAR.
A current report filed when something important happens between quarterly reports — acquisitions, CEO changes, material events.
Percent change from prior close to current price. Large gaps often follow overnight news.
How many Canadian dollars one U.S. dollar buys. Matters for trade, oil, and Canadian companies.
Oil prices affect energy stocks, airlines, inflation, and currencies like CAD. Energy shocks ripple across sectors.
Higher rates tend to hurt growth/tech and real estate; help banks; affect bond prices and borrowing costs.
Two things moving together does not prove one caused the other. Always ask for the mechanism.
A compelling narrative can still be a poor trade if the news is priced in or the risk/reward is poor.
When a model memorizes past data instead of learning patterns that generalize. It can look great in backtests and fail live.
Picking stocks at random. If AI cannot beat random over time, the system must show that honestly.
Winning one week does not prove a strategy works. Sample size and regime matter.
Simulated profit/loss using real delayed quotes — no real orders or money at risk.
NLP compound score on news headlines. Positive/negative tone — not a guarantee of price direction.
